methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Skeleton Technologies, a Series E climate and energy company founded by Taavi Madiberk, has raised $200M+ to date. The comparable universe spans 9 private firms and 1 public company (LNZA) in the Climate & Energy sector, though a sufficiently tight peer set at identical stage and valuation tier does not exist for median-based triangulation.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable and yields $280M valuation. This approach uses total capital raised ($200M) as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical for Series E advancement, reflecting the company's progression within the financing sequence. The Peer-Median method is not applicable; while 21 companies operate in the same sector, filtering for same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation tiers yields fewer than the minimum 3 datapoints required for reliable median estimation. Reliance on all-sector medians would introduce excessive noise given sector dispersion in early-stage climate tech valuations.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $238M to $322M, with a median point of $280M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression output. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow peer comparables at identical stage and tier, limiting cross-check validation, and the inherent variability in step-up multiples across different investor cohorts and market conditions at the Series E stage.
